brief.query: Generate and validate a vtk.js mag-iso scene with browser evidence vtk.js Mag-Iso Benchmark The candidate should preserve iso-surface intent while staying compatible with browser verification.
recommendedPatterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval.
Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict.
Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation.
Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order.
Inject the vtk.js browser bundle before any module script that references global vtk.
failureFixPairs: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable.
If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command.
If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first.
When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic.
If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts.
If streamline output is empty, inspect seed generation and pipeline ordering before general browser repair.
brief.generationPrompt: Generate vtk.js code for the requested scene while preserving a deterministic browser-validation path. Scene kind: mag_iso. Use these retrieval-backed patterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval. | Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict. | Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation. | Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order. | Always call renderer.resetCamera() before renderWindow.render(). | Inject the vtk.js browser bundle before any module script that references global vtk.. Avoid regressions captured by these failure-fix hints: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable. | If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command. | If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first. | When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic. | If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts. | If streamline output is empty, inspect seed generation and pipeline ordering before general browser repair.. Acceptance contract: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification. | The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render(). | Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png. | The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors. | The generated mag-iso workflow should make iso-value or contour extraction intent explicit..
acceptanceChecks: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification.
The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render().
Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png.
The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors.
The generated mag-iso workflow should make iso-value or contour extraction intent explicit.

generatedCode.summary: vtk.js generated candidate for mag_iso scene

starterNotes: This generated candidate keeps a deterministic browser-validation path by default.
The browser script is ready for Phase 5 render verification without additional bundling steps.
The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification.
The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render().
Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png.
